title: Site Manager Takeover
concept: A manager-first Playground interface where Settings, Files, Blueprint, Database, and Logs own the main workspace, while creation, saving, saved management, gallery, import, and export flows are inline supporting panels and the WordPress site is reduced to a narrow reference preview.
focus: warm editorial accents with dense controls
flows: Start a new Playground from vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, or .zip import using inline source details.
Save a temporary Playground in browser storage or to a local directory with visible file-copy progress.
Manage unsaved and saved Playgrounds with rename and delete controls.
Use Site Manager tabs for settings, files, blueprint editing, database tools, and logs while the site remains visible as a narrow preview.
Browse featured and filtered Blueprint gallery entries, then copy, download, or run the current Blueprint bundle.
Export to GitHub, download a .zip bundle, import a .zip, and navigate the active WordPress path.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
edit WordPress, PHP, and language settings
understand destructive reset and saved reload behavior
file browser with create file, create folder, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
view, copy, download, and run the current Blueprint bundle
inspect database driver, path, and size
